chunks.text: 1 BEFORE THE ELECTION APPELLATE TRIBUNAL FOR SINDH AT SUKKUR Election Appeal No. S-23 of 2024 Muhammad Sulleman son of Dhani Bux Muslim adult by caste Deeper r/o Village Khair Muhammad Deeper PS Dhareja Taluka Thari Mir Wah, District Khairpur - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Appellant V E R S U S 1. Federation of Pakistan through Chief Election Commission, Election Commission of Pakistan 2. Provincial Election Commission, Sindh, Karachi 3. District Election Commissioner, District Khairpur 4. Returning Officer NA-204 District Khairpur - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - Respondents Mr. Abdul Qadeer Khoso, Advocate for appellant Mr. Zeeshan Hyder, Law Officer, Election Commission of Pakistan Mr. Dareshani Ali Hyder ‘Ada’ DAG Mr. Umair Ali Election Officer Mr. Shan Ali Returning Officer PS-NA 204 Khairpur-III Date of hearing; 08.01.2024 Date of order; 08.01.2024 O R D E R Zulfiqar Ali Sangi J;- Through this Appeal, the appellant has challenged the impugned order dated 29.12.2023 passed by the Returning Officer NA-204 Khairpur-III, whereby his nomination form was rejected on the ground that he is a defaulter of the SEPCO for amount of Rs. 2,44,412/- The plea of appellant is that he has filed a suit for settlement of accounts in respect of false bill issued by SEPCO authorities. Since the amount alleged is admitted by the appellant to be dues against him. He has given opportunity by the Returning Officer so also by this Tribunal to clear the liability but such opportunity has not been availed by the appellant. Under such circumstances, the case of 2 appellant falls under Article 63 (o) of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973.

He has given opportunity by the Returning Officer so also by this Tribunal to clear the liability but such opportunity has not been availed by the appellant. Under such circumstances, the case of 2 appellant falls under Article 63 (o) of the Constitution of Islamic Republic of Pakistan, 1973. Hence, his nomination form was rightly rejected by the Returning Officer. Appeal fails and is hereby dismissed. J U D G E M.Ali/steno
